title: 7. “The Realities of Time” | Gene and Roger
format: Part seven of Brian Raftery's Gene and Roger series: the mid-90s thaw between Siskel and Ebert, Gene's brain-cancer diagnosis, and the last year of films he reviewed.

segments.heading: A running battle with the realities of time
segments.note: Siskel and Ebert in the 90s, and Gene's last year

films.title: Broken Arrow
films.note: The John Travolta action movie Gene half-endorsed until Roger talked him out of it, the one time in 20 years Gene flipped his own thumb on air.

films.title: Cop and a Half
films.note: The Burt Reynolds comedy Roger wanted Gene to recant on camera; Gene refused, saying he saw things in it he admired.

films.title: Saturday Night Fever
films.note: Gene auctioned off Travolta's white suit in the mid-90s, joking he'd use the proceeds toward his kids' college.

films.title: Lost in Space
films.note: Fodder for a Letterman bit where Gene kept steering back to the movie and Roger kept insisting Leonardo DiCaprio was hunky.

films.title: Independence Day
films.note: Both critics had thumbed it down; its creators' next film got even.

films.title: Godzilla
films.note: Gene's comeback title after brain surgery: he watched a screener in the hospital and phoned in his review over a still photo. The film mocked the pair with a heavyset, bald 'Mayor Ebert.'

films.title: The Truman Show
films.note: Gene watched it twice while recovering and called it a watershed, a return to films of ideas.

films.title: Bulworth
films.note: Cited alongside The Truman Show as proof the movies were getting back to ideas.

films.title: The Thin Red Line
films.note: One of the films Gene loved in his final year of reviewing.

TV: The Tonight Show with Jay Leno
